key_metrics: Diluted earnings per share of $0.52 (down from $0.77 YoY)
Research and development expenses of $2.13 billion (+9.4% YoY)
Other expense, net of $2.64 billion (+96.2% YoY)
qoq_changes: Net revenues increased sequentially to $15.42 billion in Q2 from an implied $13.34 billion in Q1 2025.
Net income declined sequentially to $938 million in Q2 from an implied $1.29 billion in Q1 2025, driven by higher non-operating expenses.
new_risks: Massive increase in 'Other expense, net' ($2.64 billion in Q2 2025 vs $1.35 billion in Q2 2024) severely dragging down GAAP profitability.
Significant earnings volatility and cash flow impact from contingent consideration liabilities, which saw a $4.31 billion fair value adjustment in H1 2025.
management_tone: Neutral; while core operating performance and top-line growth remain robust, massive non-operating expenses and contingent liability adjustments are heavily weighing on bottom-line GAAP results.
analyst_note: AbbVie demonstrated strong core operational health with a 6.6% increase in revenue and a 22.4% expansion in operating earnings. However, the 31.5% YoY drop in GAAP net income warrants close scrutiny, as it was entirely driven by a massive $2.64 billion 'Other expense' charge and ongoing fair value adjustments to contingent consideration liabilities.
